string.text: {=a9SvvCvP}Perhaps one day we shall see each other on a battlefield. You shall know the Ghilman by the splendor of our steeds and the shimmer of our steel. If we are on your side, you may rejoice. If we are your enemy, you may save yourself a great deal of trouble by running away before the fighting starts.
{=UVQ8Crg4}If you're ever looking for crossbowmen, friend, you should think about hiring the Genoese. The stoutest lads in Italy flock to our banner. Because if you don't hire us, your enemy might.
{=jrnwkG3h}If you ever find yourself in a position to hire warriors, you should consider the Almogávares. We know what it means to honor your word and contract, if the prize is right
{=WSELrtPu}The Order of Assassins are the keepers of the secret. We watch from the shadows. Make foes of us at your peril.

{=nJJ46bcj}We welcome strangers into our lands. The woods are wide, and there is game to be hunted and food to be gathered, enough for all. But there are some who come here to steal from us, to enslave us, or to tax us. Every spring, the bodies of those thieves are discovered in the thaw.
{=q28ifvwR}Maybe you have not heard of the Cumans. We do venture much into the sewn lands, the corrupt lands. We bow to no Khan. We sew no grain. We keep to the old ways, out amid the great grasses.
{=iYNgPCS5}You may have heard of us - the Knights Hospitaller at Sudtone. We are charged with healing the ill and safeguarding the roads and the bridges from brigands.
{=fA4PDFWH}You may have heard of us - the Order of Santiago. We are charged with healing the ill and safeguarding the roads and the bridges from brigands.
{=4Ze8SXpk}You may have heard of us - the Order of Saint Lazarus. We are charged with healing the ill and safeguarding the roads and the bridges from brigands.

{=AAn3TEfV}You may have heard of us. Men call us the Templars. Generations ago, Heaven instructed us to prepare for a great cleansing of the land. Until that day, we smolder in silence. But when that day comes, our righteous wrath shall burn forth.

{=stkW4AxU}You may have heard of us. Men call us the Cathars. Generations ago, Heaven instructed us to prepare for a great cleansing of the land. Until that day, we smolder in silence. But when that day comes, our righteous wrath shall burn forth.
